reasoning: This is a Commission Guidelines document (C(2025) 5052 final) issued by the European Commission on prohibited AI practices under the EU AI Act. The document is dated 29.7.2025, bears an official Commission designation, and systematically interprets and explains the binding prohibitions in Article 5 of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689. While styled as a "Communication" and "Guidelines," it serves an authoritative interpretive function for the binding legal framework established by the AI Act itself. The document's comprehensive coverage of each prohibition with detailed rationale, scope boundaries, and enforcement provisions indicates it functions as binding guidance for compliance. However, some uncertainty exists about whether this qualifies as a "binding guideline" per the narrow definition (which requires the text itself to assert binding force), versus guidance that merely explains existing binding obligations.
